info.title: Greenhouse Platform — Setpoint API (single-authority write path)
info.description: The platform Go API's single-authority setpoint write path (catalog contract #3) — the request/response contract the optimizer (Phase 3) and, later, the Phase 4 planner use to submit refined climate targets to the platform, which validates them against crop-safe bounds, records provenance (`source = optimizer`), and delivers them to the controller (RFC-005). One endpoint: `POST /api/greenhouses/{greenhouse_id}/setpoints`. The body is the same partial (merge) `SetpointsPatch` the operator's ad-hoc edit uses — that operator `PATCH` counterpart lives in the separate operator/fleet contract (`platform-dashboard-rest/`, catalog #4) — but a submission here is recorded with optimizer provenance and returned 202 Accepted. Identity, units, and timestamps follow RFC-007: the same `greenhouse_id` slug that keys MQTT topics, controller REST paths, and DB rows; field names are snake_case. Authentication is the config-gated service boundary (RFC-011): with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=trusted_network` (the default) the platform accepts the untokened internal call; with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=oidc` it requires a Keycloak client-credentials token carrying the narrow `setpoints:write` role (or an operator token) and rejects unauthenticated calls 401 / non-privileged 403. The contract is identical in both modes — only the `Authorization` header's presence differs — so `bearerAuth` is declared as an optional scheme (`security: [{}, { bearerAuth: [] }]`). A rejected write returns 422 naming the violated field and bound, mirroring the controller and operator/fleet contracts. `info.version` is the contract version; its major bumps on a breaking change, recorded in an ADR (RFC-007). This document is split: the single path is a file under `paths/`, and shared schemas, parameters, and responses live under `components/`; run `redocly bundle` to emit a single self-contained file.

servers.description: The platform's nginx entry point, which reverse-proxies `/api` to the Go API. The optimizer reaches this write path over the internal Docker network; it does not reach the controllers directly.

bearerAuth.description: Config-gated service token (RFC-011). A Keycloak client-credentials access token carrying the narrow `setpoints:write` service role (an operator token is also accepted). Required only when the platform runs with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=oidc`; with the default `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=trusted_network` the call is accepted untokened on the trusted Docker network. Declared as an optional scheme so the contract is identical in both modes — enforcement is gated on config, not on the document.
